title: Apply SKIP_DIRS filter to git-tracked files, not just filesystem walk
context: Repos that accidentally track vendored code (node_modules/, dist/) had those symbols indexed because the exclusion filter only guarded the filesystem fallback path, not the git ls-files path.
decision: Filter git-tracked files through SKIP_DIRS before processing, ensuring consistent exclusion behavior regardless of discovery path (git vs filesystem walk).
consequences: Repos with committed vendored code no longer leak symbols into the index.
Both code-discovery paths (git ls-files and filesystem walk) now apply the same exclusion rules.
Test added to prevent regression of this asymmetry.
alternatives_rejected: Keep asymmetry but document it—silent source of bugs.
Add separate allowlist for git-tracked files—adds complexity without solving root problem.
Remove git ls-files path entirely—breaks correctness for normal git repos.

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep asymmetry but document it—silent source of bugs.
Add separate allowlist for git-tracked files—adds complexity without solving root problem.
Remove git ls-files path entirely—breaks correctness for normal git repos.
